ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND OVER-RULING IN PART DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT BOB KORMAN AND NANCY RYTI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED - DEMURRER 1) CAUSE OF ACTIONS 3 AND 4 FRAUD SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD WITH PARTICULARITY; 2) CAUSE OF ACTION 9 TERMS OF CONTRACT SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD AND 2 YEAR STATUE OF LIMITATIONS NOT APPLICABLE. SUSTAIN WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND CAUSE OF ACTIONS 5, 6 AND 7 BECAUSE STATUE OF LIMITATIONS EXPIRED. TORT CAUSE OF ACTIONS ACCRUED SEPTEMBER 2009 AT THE LATEST. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY , CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT BOB KORMAN AND NANCY RYTI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled - Demurrer 1) Cause of actions 3 and 4 fraud sufficiently plead with particularity; 2) Cause of action 9 terms of contract sufficiently plead and 2 year statue of limitations not applicable. Sustain without leave to amend cause of actions 5, 6 and 7 because statue of limitations expired. Tort cause of actions accrued September 2009 at the latest. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay , Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT BOB KORMAN AND NANCY RYTI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted striking all references to Rent Ordinance in Complaint. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay , Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Russell Robinson (415-255-0462) of Law Office of Russell A. Robinson for Bob Korman, defendant and moving party; No other appearance

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: DEFENDANTs BOB KORMAN and NANCY RYTI's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is sustained without leave to amend 1st cause of action, violation of Rent Ordinance re Statute of Limitations 1 year; 2nd cause cause of action violation 1940.9 overrule; 3rd cause of action conversion/trespass, overrule, conversion sufficient; 4th and 5th causes of action, fraud and misrepresentation - sustain with leave to amend to address with particularity (who, when, where); 6th, 7th & 8th causes of action, sustain with leave to amend to address with particularity as to dates re Statue of Limitations; 9th cause of action violation of 1950.5, overrule, sufficient re rent deposit (bad faith); 10th cause of action Breach of Contract, sustain with leave to amend to attach contract and or state terms with particularity and nature; 11th cause of action conspiracy - sustain without leave to amend, cannot consipire with self.
